approved_claims: Reduces systolic BP by avg 10mmHg in 8 weeks (per FDA label)
Indicated for heart failure post-MI (SAVE trial)
Slows progression of diabetic nephropathy (EUCLID trial)
Once-daily dosing for improved patient compliance
Reduces HbA1c by 1.0-1.5% as monotherapy (UKPDS data)
First-line therapy for type 2 diabetes (ADA/EASD guidelines)
Demonstrated CV mortality reduction in overweight T2DM patients (UKPDS 34)
Weight-neutral to mildly weight-reducing vs sulfonylureas
Reduces systolic BP by 12-15mmHg at 5mg dose (ALLHAT trial)
Long half-life allows true once-daily dosing with 24h BP control
Safe in renal impairment - no dose adjustment needed
Effective as monotherapy or combined with ACE inhibitors/ARBs

indications: weight management in adults with T2DM and obesity
cardiovascular risk reduction in adults with T2DM and established ASCVD
approved_claims: Reduced major adverse cardiovascular events by 26% vs placebo in SURPASS-CARDIO (HR 0.74, 95% CI 0.61-0.89, p=0.002)
Mean weight loss of 6.8 kg at 52 weeks vs 1.2 kg with standard care (p<0.001)
HbA1c reduction of 1.8% from baseline at 40 weeks as add-on therapy
Once-weekly subcutaneous dosing with pre-filled pen
positioning_strengths: Superior weight loss vs oral GLP-1 competitors
Proven cardiovascular benefit in established ASCVD
Once-weekly dosing vs daily oral alternatives
positioning_weaknesses: GI side effects (nausea) during titration — requires protocol
Injectable route vs oral MetaboMax convenience

indications: locally advanced or metastatic NSCLC with EGFR exon 19 deletion or L858R mutation
first-line treatment in EGFR-mutation positive NSCLC
pubmed_search_terms: OncoTarget vs chemotherapy quality of life
approved_claims: Median progression-free survival 18.9 months vs 10.4 months with platinum chemotherapy (HR 0.46, p<0.001) in ONCO-STAR phase III
Objective response rate 76% vs 31% for chemotherapy (p<0.001)
Oral once-daily dosing — no infusion center visits required
Grade 3+ rash in 8.2% of patients; manageable with dose modification per protocol
positioning_strengths: High efficacy in EGFR exon 19 and L858R mutations
Oral once-daily — no IV infusion center burden
Superior quality of life vs legacy IV chemotherapy regimens
positioning_weaknesses: Strict adherence and rash monitoring required
Grade 3+ rash risk — dose modification protocol essential
Less infusion-chair visibility vs VenaChemo for adherence tracking
